examples.title: User Story Template
examples.description: Standard format for writing user stories in Agile
examples.explanation: User Stories follow a simple format: As a [role], I want [goal], so that [benefit]. This ensures the focus remains on user value.

examples.title: Daily Scrum Meeting Format
examples.description: Structure for conducting a 15-minute daily standup
examples.code:
PROCEDURE DailyScrum(teamMembers):
 FOR each member IN teamMembers:
 PRINT "What did you do yesterday?"
 PRINT member.yesterdayWork
 PRINT "What will you do today?"
 PRINT member.todayPlan
 IF member.hasBlocker THEN:
 PRINT "Blocker: " + member.blockerDescription
 ScrumMaster.raiseImpediment(member.blocker)
 END IF
 END FOR
 // Time-box: Must not exceed 15 minutes

// Team synchronizes and creates plan for next 24 hours
PRINT "Daily Scrum Complete - Time: 15 minutes max"
examples.explanation: Daily Scrum is a brief meeting where team members share progress, plans, and impediments. It ensures continuous communication.

examples.title: Sprint Planning Algorithm
examples.description: High-level steps for Sprint Planning meeting
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SprintPlanning(productBacklog, sprintDuration):
 // Step 1: Select items for Sprint
 sprintBacklog = []
 velocity = team.averageVelocity // story points per sprint
 
 WHILE sprintBacklog.storyPoints < velocity AND 
 productBacklog.hasMoreItems:
 item = productBacklog.getNextHighestPriority()
 IF item.estimated AND NOT item.completed THEN:
 sprintBacklog.add(item)
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 // Step 2: Break down into tasks
 FOR each item IN sprintBacklog:
 tasks = item.decomposeIntoTasks()
 sprintBacklog.addTasks(tasks)
 END FOR
 
 RETURN sprintBacklog
 
// Maximum time-box: 8 hours for 1-month sprint
PRINT "Sprint Goal: " + sprintBacklog.goal
examples.explanation: Sprint Planning selects items from Product Backlog based on team velocity and breaks them into tasks. It's time-boxed to ensure focus.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(k) where k = sprint backlog size

examples.title: Sprint Retrospective Process
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SprintRetrospective(team):
 // Three questions asked to each team member
 
 PRINT "What went well in this Sprint?"
 positiveFeedback = []
 FOR each member IN team:
 response = INPUT("Your positive feedback:")
 positiveFeedback.add(response)
 END FOR
 
 PRINT "What didn't go well?"
 improvements = []
 FOR each member IN team:
 response = INPUT("What needs improvement:")
 improvements.add(response)
 END FOR
 
 PRINT "What will we commit to improving in next Sprint?"
 actionItems = []
 FOR each item IN improvements:
 IF item.isActionable THEN:
 owner = INPUT("Who will take ownership?")
 actionItems.add(ActionItem(item, owner))
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN actionItems
 // Maximum time-box: 3 hours for 1-month sprint

PRINT "Sprint Retrospective Complete - Plan for next Sprint"
examples.explanation: The retrospective helps the team reflect on their process and continuously improve. It focuses on what worked, what didn't, and actionable improvements.
